summary: The business is deep in active deal flow with SP030 due diligence, Brad/Trystan's RFI process, and multiple buyer-side SBA doc threads all running in parallel as of July 7. The immediate risk is a cluster of open loops — Rick's 48-item DD response to Swetha, Morgan's outreach to Roy/Jake/Tucker, and Brad/Tonya's deposit conversation — that all need confirmation they actually happened before momentum stalls.

today: Confirm Rick sent SP030 due diligence response to Swetha (48-item list, committed 7/6 — verify now)
Confirm Morgan connected with Roy (phone tag since 7/7 — check status)
Confirm Morgan had deposit conversation with Brad and Tonya
Send Tucker email re: Welch update and business (Morgan flagged 7/7, no confirmation)
tomorrow: Follow up with Jake — read Morgan's message 7/6 but no reply; escalate if still silent
Confirm Tom's SBA docs are moving and Donnie Calvert call is scheduled
Review all July 7 open loops and mark confirmed or re-escalate
week: Brad/Trystan: Wednesday call with Trystan (9:30) + Friday mock interview — prep and execute
Chase Hal on outstanding SBA docs
Call Marc re: Roy's offer, pricing, and new deal structure
Draft contractor day-in-the-life question list; begin outreach for video project

signals: SBA documentation is a recurring chokepoint across at least two buyers (Tom, Hal) — may need a standardized chase process
Multiple communication threads (Roy, Jake, Tucker) show a pattern of messages sent but not confirmed received or acted on — confirmation discipline is a gap
Deal activity is concentrated in early July with no facts older than July 7, suggesting a recent acceleration in deal flow
Two distinct deals (SP030 and Brad/Trystan) are at structurally different stages simultaneously, stretching the two-person team's bandwidth
The contractor video project is the only non-deal initiative and risks being deprioritized unless explicitly scheduled
topRisks: SP030 momentum: if Rick's 48-item DD response was never sent, Swetha may disengage — this is the highest-stakes unconfirmed action
Phone tag with Roy has gone unresolved since at least 7/6; a deal cannot progress without live conversation — risk of losing the thread entirely
Brad/Tonya deposit not yet paid as of 7/7 — until funds are received, the deal commitment is unconfirmed
SBA doc delays across multiple buyers (Tom, Hal) could cause financing timelines to slip and delay or kill closings
bottomLine: Close the loop on every open commitment from 7/7 — Rick's SP030 due diligence response, Morgan's calls to Roy and Brad/Tonya, and the Tucker email — before any new prospecting.

quickWins.what: Rick sends his response to Swetha's 48-item due diligence list today (SP030).
quickWins.why: Swetha is engaged and waiting — a delayed response risks losing a live, prioritized deal.

quickWins.what: Morgan confirms Brad/Tonya deposit conversation happened and captures their commitment in writing.
quickWins.why: Deposit payment is a clean checkpoint to lock in buyer seriousness and move the deal forward.

quickWins.what: Morgan sends Tucker the Welch update email today.
quickWins.why: A flagged, unsent commitment from 7/7 — easy to execute and keeps Tucker warm on an active deal.

quickWins.what: Morgan connects with Roy today and loops in Marc to align on Roy's offer and deal structure.
quickWins.why: Phone tag plus an unresolved pricing/structure question with Marc means this deal is stalling on two fronts simultaneously.

longTerm.play: Build a contractor day-in-the-life video series to generate inbound seller and buyer leads.
longTerm.today: Morgan drafts the first five probe questions for contractor interviews and texts two existing contacts to gauge participation interest.

longTerm.play: Systematize SBA doc tracking so buyer financing delays stop killing deal momentum.
longTerm.today: Create a one-page SBA checklist with deadlines and assign Tom and Hal specific due dates by end of day today.

longTerm.play: Develop a repeatable mock interview process for buyers to increase FedEx approval rates.
longTerm.today: Document the agenda and talking points from Brad/Trystan's Friday mock interview to use as a template for future buyers.

longTerm.play: Establish a structured deal pricing framework so Roy-type offer/pricing conversations don't stall mid-pipeline.
longTerm.today: Schedule the Marc call this week with a written agenda covering Roy's offer, pricing benchmarks, and the new deal structure.

strategy.bottleneck: Open loops aren't being confirmed as closed — commitments made on 7/6 and 7/7 have no documented resolution; fix this by ending each day with a five-minute written status update on every open item.
leanIn: The Brad/Trystan deal has structured momentum — RFI, a Wednesday call, and a Friday mock interview — press this sequence hard as a model for future deals.
Swetha is actively engaged on SP030 with a detailed due diligence list, signaling a serious buyer worth prioritizing above colder leads.
The contractor video concept is low-cost and differentiated — no other FedEx line haul brokers are known to do this, making it a real brand moat.
stopDoing: Stop letting phone-tag cycles (Roy) drift without a fallback — if a call misses twice, send a structured written summary instead and request a scheduled time.
Stop treating the pipeline as current without auditing it — any lead with no activity before May 27 should be marked stale and removed from active tracking.
Stop initiating new outreach or content projects until the seven open 7/7 commitments are resolved and documented as done.
